name: West Virginia Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to West Virginia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
provider.parentOrganization: West Virginia Board of Examiners for SLP and Audiology

address.description:
WV Board of Examiners for SLPA
3306 Pennsylvania Ave
Suite 201
Charleston, WV 25302

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a West Virginia SLP license cost?
Is West Virginia part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. West Virginia is one of the first states actively issuing compact privileges.

mainEntity.name: Does West Virginia allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does West Virginia have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. West Virginia participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in West Virginia without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to West Virginia?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in West Virginia while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
